quote.text: In every chain of reasoning, the evidence of the last conclusion can be no greater than that of the weakest link of the chain, whatever may be the strength of the rest.
quote.author: Thomas Reid, Essays on the Intellectual Powers of Man (1786), Essay VII

macro.title: What It Means For VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

stories.kicker: The World · The Gulf War, Day 28
stories.headline: Houthis Blockade Saudi Arabia as the Gulf War Widens Further
stories.dek: A third American service member is dead and Iranian fire has now reached six Gulf states in 72 hours, as Yemen's Houthis open a new front against Riyadh in the Red Sea.

stories.headline: Faishal Ibrahim resigns as minister and MP over conduct lapse
stories.dek: A month-old email about "inappropriate interactions" ends a front-bench career within a day of reaching the Prime Minister's Office — the Home Affairs and Muslim Affairs portfolios reassigned same-day, no criminal charges brought against either party.

stories.headline: The chokepoint moved from the chip to the gas cylinder
stories.dek: China's tungsten export curbs just took a quarter of the world's high-purity chipmaking gas offline — permanently. It's a smaller, quieter lever than an export ban, and it lands at exactly the manufacturing step a GPU's own memory stack depends on.

stories.headline: Jurong's second-CBD anchor site returns — smaller, and state-subsidised
stories.dek: URA relaunches the Town Hall Link white site at roughly half its old size, with the government now absorbing the district infrastructure bill — an unusually generous fix for a 2023 mega-parcel that drew one bid, deemed too low, and no award.

stories.headline: The night before Unpacked, the watches do the talking
stories.dek: Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 and Watch 9 leaks sharpen to specific numbers hours before Wednesday's London event — the glasses stay the quieter, unconfirmed story until then.

stories.headline: The Matter server catches up to the spec it just chased
stories.dek: A beta already ships full Matter 1.6 support barely a month after ratification — and the pylutron crash flagged last issue traces to a fix that's already merged.

stories.headline: A flagship season for high-end audio, while two watch-auction results stay genuinely unresolved
stories.dek: McIntosh, Ortofon and Marantz all shipped landmark components this fortnight — Sotheby's Singapore, closed four days ago, still hasn't published a number.

stories.headline: Apple TV+ takes Hall H for the first time, as San Diego Comic-Con opens to a genuinely new headliner
stories.dek: Preview night is tomorrow; by Saturday, a streamer with no Marvel or Star Wars behind it is running the convention's biggest room solo.

stories.headline: Roblox's settlement math: five states, one mediator
stories.dek: General guidance, not legal or medical advice. What was one state's settlement last issue is now a five-state pattern — and the federal case has quietly acquired a mediator.

stories.headline: From the wellhead to the tap
stories.dek: Iran's war on Gulf energy widens to Kuwait's drinking water and stalls Hormuz shipping; Chair Warsh's hawkish week repriced the 28–29 July FOMC; gold's war-hedge decoupling holds rather than reverses. Market explanation and education, not investment advice — forecasts are flagged as forecasts throughout.

stories.headline: Breathing room
stories.dek: A Singapore–Japan lab teaches cockroaches to dive; a bristle worm's jaw turns out to be a material science hasn't named yet; and three bones filed away for thirty years become a new giant salamander.

stories.headline: An algorithm wants to pick who you fall for. The internet noticed.
stories.dek: Hinge's founder built a matchmaker with no swiping and no profiles. The backlash arrived faster than the product will.

stories.headline: Your handshake might be buffering your brain
stories.dek: General guidance, not medical advice. A new grip-strength finding is genuinely interesting — and easy to over-read.

stories.headline: The October Squeeze
stories.dek: A Grand Prix weekend, a stadium K-pop date, two national festival peaks and a Test match all land within six weeks of each other — book the flights before the hotels do.

stories.headline: The Narrowest Point
stories.dek: A strait, a manufacturing gas, a single desalination plant, one email: this week's news kept turning on parts too small or too boring to have been defended — the places a system is actually weakest. Three episodes, two thousand years apart, about what happens when someone finds the narrow point first.
